title: ibervillea millspaughii
intro: Ibervillea millspaughii, commonly known as the Devil's Claw, is a plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It is a perennial vine with tuberous roots, which are used for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Ibervillea millspaughii include its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it effective in treating conditions such as arthritis, gout, and other musculoskeletal disorders. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, triterpenes, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions, including analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and anti-arthritic effects.

parts: Ibervillea millspaughii has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which have been traditionally used for their therapeutic properties. The roots of Ibervillea millspaughii are rich in compounds like ibervin, a lignan, and other phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The flowers of Ibervillea millspaughii contain glycosides and other compounds that may have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, although more research is needed to confirm these properties.
preparations: Ibervillea millspaughii has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that are used for medicinal purposes. The tea made from Ibervillea millspaughii is said to have antioxidant properties and is used to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues. The tincture of Ibervillea millspaughii is often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. side_effects: Ibervillea millspaughii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal problems, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Prolonged use or excessive consumption may also lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue, resulting from the plant's stimulant properties.
